threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'tote bag' instead of a 'shopping bag'. Proper Hackney folk have that local twang about their carry.
Caught the N70 last night; a couple was arguing over who forgot the takeaway. The bus driver just kept playing his tunes loud, didn't even flinch.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sipping coffee at a café, I heard a group debate the best Döner in town—nothing says Neukölln like passionate food fights over takeout.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, there’s an open-air cinema that shows classic films in the summer, complete with blankets and beer - it’s like a festival every weekend!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name the taco spot that used to be a laundromat on Mission and share stories of the mural artists who painted it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a street performer leading a full Muni bus in a spontaneous dance today—only in the Tenderloin do commuters groove through rush hour!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that most locals don’t drink coffee standing up. We prefer to sit down and enjoy it, soaking up the ambiance in our favourite bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they hesitate at the tram stops, glancing at their phones like they’re deciphering a cryptic message.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'sandwich' when you order from the local shops. It's a 'bacon sarnie' here, mate. Little things matter!
Last night on the 35, a couple argued over a missing shoe. Turns out, it was under the seat the whole time. Classic Brixton chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid in a schnitzel costume riding a skateboard past a café while their parents sipped kombucha. It's a unique blend here.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a laundromat that doubles as a bar. Sip a Kölsch while waiting for your clothes—only in Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to avoid BART Civic Center on weekends—loud bangs and smoke are just part of the charm, but the evacs are a hassle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today where a guy was negotiating the fare with a bag of peanuts. Only in the TL could that be normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just the Gaudí sights, but the real charm is the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut and chat for hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their oversized backpacks and the way they hesitate at every metro entrance, like it's a labyrinth.
